--- engine: knitr --- # Krona
![](/images/krona.png){style="width:150px; background: white; border-radius:5px; border: white solid 5px"}
`Krona` is an interactive metagenome species abundance visualisation tool. We will use it to produce interactive tiered pie charts from our `Kraken2` output. ## Import taxonomy
![](/images/play_red.png){style="width:100px; background: white; border-radius:5px; border: white solid 5px"}
We can use the `Kraken2` report files to create our Krona plots. With the below command we can import our `Kraken2` taxonomy (within the report file) into a `Krona` html. ::: {.cell} ```{.bash .cell-code} ktImportTaxonomy -o kraken2.krona.html *.kreport2 ``` ::: - `-o` is our output html file - The final argument `*.kreport2` represents all of our `.kreport2` files in the current directory - The `*` is a wild-card, meaning any characters any number of times. Therefore `*.kreport2` identifies the files `K1.kreport2` `K2.kreport2` and `W1.kreport2` You will get a warning that not all taxonomy IDs were found. We will ignore this but in your own future installations this should be addressed with `Krona`'s `updateTaxonomy.sh` command. ## Tiered piecharts
![](/images/eye_red.png){style="width:100px; background: white; border-radius:5px; border: white solid 5px"}
We can view our interactive chart in a web browser. This shows the percentage of reads that were classified to various taxonomies at different levels. ::: {.cell} ```{.bash .cell-code} firefox kraken2.krona.html & ``` ::: This is an interactive multi-tiered pie chart with many options. Some of the interactivity is described below: - To choose a sample to view, click on the sample name in the top left - To zoom into a lower taxonomy, double click on the taxonomy's name on the pie chart - E.g. double click the word "Bacteria" on the pie chart to zoom into Bacteria and therefore ignore Eukaryota, and "[other Root]" - To zoom out to a higher taxonomy, click on the taxonomy's name in the middle of the pie chart - E.g. click the word "root" in the middle of the pie chart to zoom back out from any lower level of taxonomy - To view percentage stats of a taxonomy, click on the taxonomy name on the pie chart. - This will display the percentage this taxonomy covers of all the different taxonomies above it that it belongs to. This is displayed on the right side of the screen - E.g. Click on Pseudomonadota to see the percentage of the Root it accounts for and to see the percentage of Bacteria it accounts for in the sample ## MCQs
![](/images/question_bubble_red.png){style="width:100px; background: white; border-radius:5px; border: white solid 5px"}
Viewing the Krona, attempt the below MCQs. ::: {.cell} ::: 1. What percentage of the __root__ was classified to __Bacteria__ for __K1__?
::: {.cell} ::: 2. What percentage of __bacteria__ was classified to __Pseudomonadota__ for __K2__?
::: {.cell} ::: 3. What percentage of the __root__ was classified to __Myxococcota__ for __W1__?
::: {.cell} ::: 4. What percentage of the __Terrabacteria groups__ was classified to __Cellulomonas gilvus__ for __K2__?
::: {.cell} ::: 5. What percentage of __Alphaproteobacteria__ was classified to __Phenylobacterium immobile__ for __K1__?
::: {.cell} ::: 6. What percentage of __Viridiplantae__ (in Eukaryota) was classified to __Parasponia__ for __W1__?
